summary: Do not ship yet: Undo can corrupt persisted materialization state after profile deletion, and CSP validation does not meet the promised accessibility contract or test coverage.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: Undo after profile deletion restores orphaned materialized data
findings.body: The Undo callback remains actionable after the containing profile can be deleted. `restoreModification` silently fails to insert the rule when `profileId` no longer exists, but still writes `materializedValue` into global state. This leaves an unreachable placeholder value persisted indefinitely and violates the atomic restoration contract. The committed N20 test covers only immediate Undo, so it misses this intervening-state failure.
findings.recommendation: Make restore a no-op or return an error unless the target profile still exists, and never restore materialized data without restoring the rule. Add a command-level test for remove rule → remove profile → Undo.

findings.title: CSP validation omits the required aria-invalid state
findings.body: When every CSP directive name is empty, Save is blocked and a group-level error is rendered, but none of the directive-name inputs receives `aria-invalid`. This only partially implements the spec's explicit contract that missing fields receive both inline errors and `aria-invalid`. The advertised smoke evidence checks `aria-invalid` only for an empty header name, so it passes without covering this required CSP seam.
findings.recommendation: Set `aria-invalid` on the CSP directive-name inputs when the `directives` error is active, associate the error text via `aria-describedby`, and extend smoke coverage to verify the CSP path and unchanged storage.

next_steps: Guard `restore-modification` against a missing target profile and test the intervening profile-deletion scenario.
Complete and smoke-test CSP validation accessibility before merge.
